title: Atomic Atlas: decompose Sky Atlas.md into one-folder-per-document tree + sync tooling
body:
## Summary

This is the cutover PR for atomic Atlas. Replaces the \`Sky Atlas/Sky Atlas.md\` monolith with a one-folder-per-document tree under \`content/\`, and ships the round-trippable sync tooling (\`sync/decompose.py\`, \`sync/compose.py\`, tests) that produced it.

**Stacks on PR #235** — this branch is based on the structural-fix tip (\`bb937597\`). The diff against main includes #235's changes; will rebase on \`main\` once #235 merges.

## What's in this PR

- \`sync/decompose.py\` — monolith → folder tree (548 lines).
- \`sync/compose.py\` — folder tree → monolith (391 lines).
- \`sync/test_decompose.py\` + \`sync/test_compose.py\` — 76 tests, full Atlas roundtrip byte-identical.
- \`sync/README.md\` — design notes, doc-format conventions, tooling usage.
- \`content/\` — the decomposed Atlas (10,195 documents). Generated by \`decompose.py\` from the current monolith; \`compose.py\` reproduces the monolith byte-for-byte.

## Verification

\`\`\`
cd sync && python3 -m pytest -q
# 76 passed in 0.43s

python3 sync/compose.py --input content/ --output /tmp/composed.md
diff -q "Sky Atlas/Sky Atlas.md" /tmp/composed.md
# (no output — byte-identical)
\`\`\`

## Why decomposition

- **Granular review** — single-document PRs instead of monolith diffs.
- **Programmatic editing** — atomic edits target one file's frontmatter + content rather than navigating a 200K-line monolith.
- **Format flexibility** — frontmatter encodes structure (UUID, doc_no, parent, targets), content stays markdown.
- **Tooling demonstrations** — see \`adamgfraser/next-gen-atlas:proposed/atomic-atlas\` for the live working tree.

## Merge sequencing

This PR is the format flip on the public repo. The full Phase 6 cutover requires:
1. **PR #235 lands first** — the structural fix that this branch is based on.
2. **Migration script ships** (separate PR, in flight) — translates in-flight Atlas Edit branches to decomposed format so authors don't manually rebase.
3. **Team freeze window** — short coordination window where in-flight PRs get migrated.
4. **Public + private cutover PRs merge together.**

Not a "merge whenever green" PR — needs the freeze coordination. Holding ready-for-review until the migration script is verified and a freeze window is set.

## Open follow-ups (out of scope for this PR)

- Migration script (\`sync/migrate-branches.py\`) for in-flight PR translation — separate PR.
- Tooling/CI updates (validate-atlas workflow, sync-public workflow, atlas-portal, atlas-review renderer) — separate PRs per the Phase 3 audit.
- Comment-anchor backfill on atlas-review (legacy line-number references) — separate piece.

author: adamgfraser
